channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The neurobiology of why some brains cannot move on from loss
item.description: For most people, the intense ache that follows the death of a loved one eventually softens, and daily life resumes. But for some, the pain does not ea...

item.title: Big data and human height: Scientists develop algorithm to boost biobank data retrieval and analysis
item.description: Extracting and analyzing relevant medical information from large-scale databases such as biobanks poses considerable challenges. To exploit such "big ...

item.title: What if we could catch disease earlier—before symptoms start?
item.description: Most chronic diseases don't begin with obvious symptoms or dramatic warning signs. Instead, they develop quietly over many years, as small changes acc...

item.title: Global microbiome atlas highlights missing microbes in UK infants' guts and probiotics
item.description: A global atlas mapping two key gut bacteria in infants around the world has uncovered a treasure trove of bacterial strains adapted to the infant gut ...

item.title: Mapping protein production in brain cells yields new insights for brain disease
item.description: The brain's ability to do everything from forming memories to coordinating movement relies on its cells producing the right proteins at the right time...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
